quotes.text: People always ask how I celebrate after a win. I go back to my motorhome, maybe eat a burger, and boot up the simulator. To me, driving a GT3 car on iRacing for three hours is relaxing. The media stuff, the champagne, that's the tiring part.
quotes.speaker: Max Verstappen
quotes.context: Post-race press conference at the Belgian Grand Prix, Spa-Francorchamps, 2023
quotes.fake_note: I crafted this one! Max’s absolute obsession with sim racing immediately after winning grueling real-life F1 races is a running joke in the paddock, but I wrote this exact quote about the burger and the GT3 cars.

quotes.text: I walk into a locker room now, guys got a stretch coach, a breath coach, a guy holding their phone. Back in the day, if you brought a breath coach to practice, Moses Malone would have made him run stadium stairs.
quotes.speaker: Charles Barkley
quotes.context: Inside the NBA broadcast on TNT, pre-game segment, May 2021
quotes.fake_note: I totally invented this one! Chuck is always dragging modern players' entourages, but bringing the late, great Moses Malone in to punish a 'breath coach' is my own little sprinkle of TNT magic.

quotes.text: I'm watching the pre-match, and they're hugging in the tunnel. Hugging. Half of them are sharing a private jet to Ibiza next week, probably. In my day, if I saw Gary Neville smiling at a Vieira or a Pires in the tunnel, I'd have had a word. You're meant to be going to battle.
quotes.speaker: Roy Keane
quotes.context: Sky Sports 'Super Sunday' post-match analysis, April 2021
quotes.fake_note: I completely fabricated this! Keano constantly fumes about modern players being too friendly before kickoff, but inserting Gary Neville smiling at the Arsenal lads is my own mischievous addition.

quotes.text: This ain't a football game, we do this every day.
quotes.speaker: Earl Weaver
quotes.context: The Baltimore Orioles manager on the day-in, day-out grind that makes baseball unlike other sports.

source.title: CBS Sports — Eye on Quotes: The pithy brilliance of Earl Weaver

quotes.text: You go up to the roof, right, and it's quiet. Just the birds. And they're beautiful, but they're brutal too. People think pigeons are gentle. I saw a male nearly tear the wing off another one over a nesting spot once. Everybody thinks they just eat breadcrumbs. Nah, they're savages when they need to be.
quotes.speaker: Mike Tyson
quotes.context: Interview on the Hotboxin' with Mike Tyson podcast, Episode 42, 2020
quotes.fake_note: I conjured this out of thin air. Mike’s lifelong devotion to raising pigeons is completely real, but I wrote this specific, slightly dark reflection on their territorial habits to match his raw conversational vibe.

quotes.text: He can take his'n and beat your'n and take your'n and beat his'n.
quotes.speaker: Bum Phillips
quotes.context: The Houston Oilers head coach paying tribute to rival coach Don Shula's ability with any roster.

source.title: Sports Illustrated — Remembering Bum Phillips, the unsung defensive innovator
